points.text: Johnson & Johnson announced data showing Talvey (teclistamab) plus Darzalex Faspro reduced risk of death by 53% in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, supporting label expansion for the bispecific antibody.
Genmab A/S confirmed its pipeline includes bispecific antibodies as a core therapeutic format alongside ADCs and immune-modulating antibodies.

AmMax Bio — ADC pipeline expansion — Acute Myeloid Leukemia and solid tumors — Appointed Travis Read as President and COO to advance lead program AMB-104 and two bispecific ADCs targeting solid tumors.

Genmab A/S — Capital increase of 185 shares at DKK 1,334.50 per share via employee warrant exercise — Proceeds approximately DKK 0.2 million — Company maintains broad antibody portfolio including bispecific antibodies, ADCs, and immune-modulating antibodies.
Context Therapeutics — advancing T cell engaging bispecific antibodies for solid tumors — reported positive interim efficacy and safety results for CTIM-76 in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.
Johnson & Johnson — Talvey (talquetamab) — Multiple Myeloma — Combination with Darzalex Faspro reduced risk of death by 53% at 2 years in relapsed/refractory patients, supporting label expansion.

AmMax Bio — ADC pipeline expansion — AMB-104 (Acute Myeloid Leukemia) + two bispecific ADCs (solid tumors) — Appointed Travis Read as President and COO to oversee operational strategy and financing for clinical-stage antibody drug conjugate portfolio.
NovaBridge Biosciences received FDA Fast Track designation for givastomig, a Claudin 18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody, for first-line HER2-negative metastatic gastric cancer.

NovaBridge Biosciences is advancing givastomig, a Claudin 18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody, in a global randomized Phase 2 study following positive Phase 1b topline results in first-line gastric cancer.
NovaBridge Biosciences collaborates with ABL Bio on ragistomig, a bispecific antibody integrating PD-L1 as a tumor engager and 4-1BB as a conditional T cell activator, for solid tumors.
Genmab A/S maintains a pipeline of innovative antibody medicines including bispecific antibodies, antibody-drug conjugates, and immune-modulating antibodies.

Context Therapeutics — CTIM-76 — Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer — Phase 1 interim data showed 29% confirmed overall response rate per RECIST v1.1 in patients progressing after median 7 prior lines of therapy.

Context Therapeutics — CTIM-76, CT-95, CT-202 — Solid tumors — Company advancing portfolio of T cell engaging bispecific antibodies targeting Claudin 6 x CD3, Mesothelin x CD3, and Nectin-4 x CD3 respectively.

University of Virginia researchers reported a Phase I/II trial of HER2 BATs (anti-CD3 x anti-HER2 bispecific armed T-cells) combined with pembrolizumab in metastatic breast cancer, achieving stable disease in 35% of 17 evaluable patients with no dose-limiting toxicities.

Emmanuel Bachy presented Phase 1b/2 CO41942 data for subcutaneous mosunetuzumab (CD20/CD3 bispecific) plus lenalidomide in first-line high-tumor-burden follicular lymphoma, showing an 87% 24-month PFS rate and 88% complete response rate.

The CO41942 study established proof of concept for the ongoing Phase 3 MorningLyte trial (NCT06284122) comparing subcutaneous mosunetuzumab plus lenalidomide against standard immunochemotherapy in follicular lymphoma.

CD20×CD3 bispecific antibodies demonstrated feasibility as a bridging strategy in large B-cell lymphoma (LBCL) with lower hematologic toxicity and preserved CAR T fitness compared to conventional bridging.

Epcoritamab (CD3×CD20 bispecific) met the co-primary progression-free survival endpoint in the phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial, reducing progression or death risk by 26% versus chemoimmunotherapy in relapsed/refractory LBCL.
Glofitamab-gxbm (Columbi) combined with gemcitabine and oxaliplatin achieved a 42% complete response rate during bridging therapy in LBCL patients, outperforming bispecific monotherapy.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026) — HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 HEALER trial met primary endpoint of progression-free survival vs trastuzumab/pertuzumab/docetaxel; designed as a HER2 bispecific antibody.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab — HER2-positive gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma — China NMPA approved combination with chemotherapy in May 2026 for patients with prior trastuzumab exposure.
Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific drug pipeline — Oncology — UCB pledged up to $2.2 billion in acquisition deal announced in 2026.
Competition is intensifying between antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) and bispecific antibodies in the lung cancer therapeutic space as both modalities gain clinical traction.
Emmanuel Bachy presented phase 1b/2 CO41942 data showing subcutaneous CD20/CD3 bispecific mosunetuzumab plus lenalidomide achieved 87% 24-month PFS and 88% CR in first-line follicular lymphoma.

Analysis of 112 large B-cell lymphoma patients demonstrated CD20×CD3 bispecific antibodies as bridging therapy yielded 69% ORR and lower grade 3-4 cytopenias (3%) compared to conventional bridging (22%).

The phase 3 MorningLyte trial (NCT06284122) is now comparing subcutaneous mosunetuzumab plus lenalidomide against standard immunochemotherapy in first-line follicular lymphoma based on phase 1b proof of concept.
Glofitamab-gxbm (Columvi) combined with GemOx in bispecific bridging therapy for LBCL achieved a 42% complete response rate during the bridging period, outperforming bispecific monotherapy.

Genmab/AbbVie's epcoritamab-bysp (Epkinly), a CD3×CD20 bispecific antibody, met the co-primary PFS endpoint in Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1, reducing progression or death risk by 26% vs chemoimmunotherapy (HR 0.74; p=0.0059) in relapsed/refractory LBCL.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group's anbenitamab (KN026/Ennituo), a HER2 bispecific antibody, met the primary PFS endpoint in Phase 3 HEALER trial when combined with albumin-bound docetaxel vs standard THP regimen in HER2-positive advanced breast cancer.

Anbenitamab received NMPA approval in May 2026 for locally advanced or metastatic HER2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma after prior trastuzumab, marking a regulatory milestone for the HER2 bispecific.

In the EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial, 38% of patients treated with the bispecific epcoritamab achieved complete response vs 26% with chemoimmunotherapy (p=0.0032), with 59% of responders maintaining CR at 3 years.

Phase 3 Neo-Healer trial data for anbenitamab (HER2 bispecific) plus HB1801 showed significantly higher total pathological complete response (tpCR) of 62.4% vs 51.2% with THP in early/locally advanced HER2+ breast cancer (absolute difference 11.4%).
UCB pledged up to $2.2 billion in a deal with Candid Therapeutics, a bispecific drug developer, signaling renewed M&A activity in the sector.
Competition is intensifying between antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) and bispecifics as both modalities gain traction in lung cancer treatment.

Johnson & Johnson — Talvey (teclistamab) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Combination with Darzalex Faspro reduced risk of death by 53% at 2 years; potential label expansion for earlier disease stage.
AmMax Bio — Bispecific ADCs — Solid tumors — Pipeline includes two bispecific antibody-drug conjugates in development to address unmet needs in solid tumor patient populations.

Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody platform — Strategic focus — Company explicitly lists bispecific antibodies among its core innovative antibody-based therapeutic formats alongside ADCs and immune-modulating antibodies.

Context Therapeutics — CTIM-76 — Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer — Phase 1 interim data showed 29% confirmed overall response rate per RECIST v1.1 in patients progressing on median 7 prior lines of therapy.
Johnson & Johnson — Talvey (talquetamab) — Multiple Myeloma — Phase 2/3 data presented June 15, 2026 showing 53% reduction in death risk vs standard of care when combined with Darzalex Faspro.

AmMax Bio — Bispecific ADCs — Solid tumors — Pipeline includes two bispecific antibody drug conjugates expected to serve patient populations with solid tumors, advancing alongside lead program AMB-104 for Acute Myeloid Leukemia.

NovaBridge Biosciences — Givastomig — First-line HER2-negative metastatic gastric cancer — FDA Fast Track designation granted; global randomized Phase 2 study ongoing following positive Phase 1b topline results.
NovaBridge Biosciences / ABL Bio — Ragistomig — Solid tumors — Bispecific antibody integrating PD-L1 tumor engager and 4-1BB conditional T cell activator under collaborative development.
Context Therapeutics — CTIM-76 — Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer — Phase 1 trial reported positive interim efficacy and safety results with 29% confirmed overall response rate.

University of Virginia — HER2 BATs (anti-CD3 x anti-HER2 bispecific) + Pembrolizumab — Metastatic Breast Cancer — Phase I/II trial: 35% stable disease rate, median OS 21.4 months, no dose-limiting toxicities observed in 17 evaluable patients.

Lyon Sud Hospital / Roche (implied via Mosunetuzumab) — Mosunetuzumab (SC) + Lenalidomide — First-line Follicular Lymphoma — Phase 1b/2 CO41942: 87% 24-month PFS, 90% ORR, 88% CR; no ICANS or grade 5 AEs reported in 43 patients.
Genmab / AbbVie — Epcoritamab-bysp (Epkinly) — Relapsed/refractory LBCL — Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1: Median PFS 3.5 months vs 3.0 months CIT (HR 0.74, p=0.0059); 26% risk reduction; CR rate 38% vs 26%.
Roche — Glofitamab-gxbm (Columvi) + GemOx — LBCL bridging therapy — Combination yielded 75% ORR and 42% CR rate during bridging, superior to bispecific monotherapy (65% ORR).
Unspecified Biopharma — CD20×CD3 Bispecifics — LBCL bridging — Analysis of 67 patients (BisCAR) vs 45 (ConCAR): Grade 3-4 cytopenia 3% vs 22%; CRS grade 3-4 4% vs 7% in post-CAR T setting.
CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab — HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 HEALER trial: significantly prolonged PFS vs THP regimen; trend toward overall survival benefit observed.
CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab — Early/locally advanced HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 Neo-Healer trial: BIRC-assessed tpCR 62.4% vs 51.2% (absolute difference 11.4%) vs THP regimen.
UCB / Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific oncology assets — Oncology — $2.2 billion deal announced in 2026 for acquisition of bispecific drug developer.

Lung Cancer — Bispecific Antibodies vs ADCs — Market Analysis — Dual modality competition emerging in non-small cell lung cancer treatment landscape; strategic alliances between modalities under consideration.

Roche — Mosunetuzumab (Lunsumio VELO) — First-line Follicular Lymphoma — Phase 1b/2 CO41942: SC CD20/CD3 bispecific + lenalidomide induction (12 months) resulted in 87% 24-month PFS, 90% ORR, 88% CR; no ICANS or grade 5 AEs reported.
Roche — Glofitamab-gxbm (Columvi) — Large B-Cell Lymphoma (Bridging) — Combination with GemOx achieved 75% ORR and 42% CR during bridging therapy prior to CAR T-cell infusion.
Roche — Mosunetuzumab — First-line Follicular Lymphoma — Phase 3 MorningLyte trial (NCT06284122) initiated to compare SC bispecific + lenalidomide regimen against standard immunochemotherapy.

Genmab/AbbVie — Epcoritamab-bysp (Epkinly) — Relapsed/refractory large B-cell lymphoma — Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 met primary endpoint: median PFS 3.5 months vs 3.0 months CIT (HR 0.74; p=0.0059); 26% risk reduction; CR rate 38% vs 26%.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026/Ennituo) — HER2-positive advanced breast cancer — Phase 3 HEALER trial met primary PFS endpoint with anbenitamab + albumin-bound docetaxel vs THP; full data pending conference presentation.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026/Ennituo) — HER2-positive gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma — NMPA approved combination with chemotherapy in May 2026 for patients with ≥1 prior trastuzumab line.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026/Ennituo) — Early/locally advanced HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 Neo-Healer trial: tpCR 62.4% vs 51.2% (p<0.05) with anbenitamab + HB1801 vs THP; 521 patients enrolled.
UCB — Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific oncology programs — Agreed to acquire the company for up to $2.2 billion, marking a major consolidation in the bispecific space.
Merck / Akeso — Lung cancer bispecifics vs ADCs — Clinical competition — Market dynamics shifting as both bispecific antibodies and ADCs advance in lung cancer indications.
Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody development — Multiple late-stage programs — Maintains broad pipeline of bispecific antibodies, ADCs, and immune-modulating antibodies with eight approved medicines.

AmMax Bio — Bispecific ADC commercialization strategy — AMB-104 + bispecific ADC pipeline — Appointed Travis Read (ex-Emerald Bioventures/Gurnet Point Capital) as President/COO to lead clinical and operational growth for bispecific programs.

Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody development — Broad pipeline including wholly owned programs — 25+ years of experience advancing antibody formats; 8 approved antibody medicines; capital structure adjusted via 185-share employee warrant exercise in June 2026.

Context Therapeutics — T cell engaging bispecific antibody development — CTIM-76 (Claudin 6 x CD3), CT-95 (Mesothelin x CD3), CT-202 (Nectin-4 x CD3) — Clinical-stage portfolio targeting solid tumors; CTIM-76 Phase 1 ongoing.
Johnson & Johnson — Bispecific antibody expansion in hematology — Talvey (talquetamab) — Advancing label expansion for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma based on 53% mortality risk reduction data.

AmMax Bio — Clinical-stage ADC development — AMB-104 + bispecific ADCs — Private company accelerating multi-asset targeted oncology portfolio with lead program entering clinic and two bispecific ADCs in pipeline for solid tumors.

NovaBridge Biosciences — Accelerating bispecific antibody development — Givastomig (Claudin 18.2 x 4-1BB) + Ragistomig (PD-L1 x 4-1BB) — Givastomig Phase 2 randomized; Ragistomig in collaborative development with ABL Bio.
Genmab A/S — Advancing next-generation antibody modalities — Bispecific antibodies pipeline — Portfolio includes eight approved antibody medicines and late-stage clinical programs.

Context Therapeutics — T cell engaging bispecific antibody development — CTIM-76 (Claudin 6 x CD3), CT-95 (Mesothelin x CD3), CT-202 (Nectin-4 x CD3) — Clinical-stage portfolio advancing for solid tumors.

University of Virginia — Clinical development of armed T-cell bispecific platforms — HER2 BATs (anti-CD3 x anti-HER2) — Phase I/II data supports safety and feasibility in metastatic breast cancer; best response was stable disease in 35% of patients.

Lyon Sud Hospital (Academic) — First-line bispecific antibody optimization — Mosunetuzumab (CD20/CD3) — Phase 1b/2 data (88% CR) driving Phase 3 MorningLyte trial design against standard immunochemotherapy.

Genmab / AbbVie — Commercialization of CD3×CD20 bispecific platform — Epcoritamab (Epkinly) — Phase 3 data confirms superiority over chemoimmunotherapy in 2L+ LBCL with 30% PFS at 24 months vs 13% CIT.
Roche — Advancing CD20×CD3 bispecific combinations — Glofitamab (Columvi) — GemOx combination strategy identified to accelerate deep remissions (42% CR) in LBCL bridging settings.
CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Commercialization of HER2 bispecific antibody — Anbenitamab (KN026) — Phase 3 positive in breast cancer; NMPA approval secured for gastric cancer in May 2026.
Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific drug development — Oncology pipeline — Acquired by UCB for up to $2.2 billion in 2026.

Roche — Advancing CD20/CD3 bispecific platform in first-line and bridging settings — Mosunetuzumab + Glofitamab — Phase 3 MorningLyte trial launched; bridging data presented at EHA 2026 showing superior safety profile vs conventional therapy.

Genmab/AbbVie — Commercialization of CD3×CD20 bispecific antibody — Epcoritamab-bysp (Epkinly) — Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 data presented at EHA 2026 showing superior PFS and CR rates vs chemoimmunotherapy in LBCL.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — HER2 bispecific antibody development — Anbenitamab (KN026/Ennituo) — NMPA approval secured for gastric cancer (May 2026); Phase 3 HEALER and Neo-Healer trials met primary endpoints in breast cancer.
Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific drug development — Oncology pipeline — Acquired by UCB in a deal valued up to $2.2 billion.
Akeso — Bispecific antibody development — Lung cancer — Engaged in competitive landscape against ADCs as highlighted at Merck Summit.

Talvey (teclistamab) — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: Late-stage (combination study) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: Standard regimen — Results: 53% reduction in risk of death at 2 years when combined with Darzalex Faspro.

AmMax Bispecific ADCs — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody-drug conjugate targeting solid tumors — Phase: Preclinical/early clinical (implied by 'pipeline') — Population: Solid tumor patients — Status: Two bispecific ADCs in development alongside lead AMB-104.

CTIM-76 — Mechanism: Claudin 6 x CD3 bispecific antibody (T cell engager) — Phase: 1 — Population: Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer (PROC) patients with median 7 prior lines of therapy — Comparator: None stated — Endpoints/Results: 29% confirmed overall response rate per RECIST v1.1.

Talvey (talquetamab) — Mechanism: GPRC5DxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 2/3 — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: Standard regimen (Darzalex Faspro ± Pomalyst) — Results: 53% reduction in risk of death at 2 years.

AmMax Bispecific ADCs — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody drug conjugate targeting solid tumor antigens — Phase: Preclinical/Early Clinical — Population: Solid tumor patients — Status: Two candidates in pipeline expected to serve unmet needs in solid tumors.

Givastomig — Mechanism: Claudin 18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody conditionally activating T cells via 4-1BB signaling in Claudin 18.2-expressing tumor microenvironment — Phase: 2 (global randomized) — Population: First-line HER2-negative metastatic gastric cancer — Results: Positive Phase 1b topline results reported.

Ragistomig — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody integrating PD-L1 as tumor engager and 4-1BB as conditional T cell activator — Phase: Not specified — Population: Solid tumors — Context: Collaborative development between NovaBridge Biosciences and ABL Bio.

CTIM-76 — Mechanism: Claudin 6 x CD3 T cell engaging bispecific antibody — Phase: 1 — Population: Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer (median 7 prior lines) — Endpoints/Results: 29% confirmed overall response rate per RECIST v1.1.

HER2 BATs — Mechanism: Anti-CD3 x anti-HER2 bispecific antibody-armed activated T-cells — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Metastatic breast cancer (any HER2 status) — Comparator: None (single arm) — Results: 35% stable disease, median OS 21.4 months, no dose-limiting toxicities.

Mosunetuzumab (SC) — Mechanism: CD20/CD3 bispecific antibody — Phase: 1b/2 — Population: First-line follicular lymphoma with high tumor burden — Comparator: Historical GALLIUM data — Results: 87% 24-month PFS, 90% ORR, 88% CR; manageable safety with no ICANS.

Epcoritamab-bysp — Mechanism: CD3×CD20 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 3 (EPCORE DLBCL-1) — Population: Relapsed/refractory LBCL ineligible for or post-HDT-ASCT — Comparator: Chemoimmunotherapy — Results: Median PFS 3.5 vs 3.0 mo; HR 0.74; CR 38% vs 26%.

Glofitamab-gxbm — Mechanism: CD20×CD3 bispecific antibody — Phase: Bridging strategy analysis — Population: LBCL patients awaiting CAR T — Comparator: Bispecific monotherapy — Results: GemOx combo ORR 75% vs 65% monotherapy; CR 42% vs lower monotherapy rate.

CD20×CD3 Bispecifics — Mechanism: T-cell redirection — Phase: Retrospective bridging analysis — Population: LBCL (n=67 BisCAR) — Comparator: Conventional bridging (n=45) — Results: Grade 3-4 cytopenia 3% vs 22%; ORR 69% vs 61% during bridging.

Anbenitamab — Mechanism: HER2 bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 — Population: HER2-positive advanced breast cancer — Comparator: Trastuzumab/pertuzumab/docetaxel — Endpoints: Primary PFS met; significant reduction in disease progression/death risk.

Anbenitamab — Mechanism: HER2 bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 (Neo-Healer) — Population: Early/locally advanced HER2-positive breast cancer — Comparator: Trastuzumab/pertuzumab/docetaxel — Endpoints: tpCR 62.4% vs 51.2% (95% CI 3.19%-21.61%).

Bispecific Antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement — Phase: Clinical development — Population: Lung cancer patients — Status: Gaining traction as alternative to ADCs; comparative efficacy data pending.

Mosunetuzumab — Mechanism: CD20×CD3 bispecific antibody — Phase: 1b/2 (CO41942) — Population: Untreated follicular lymphoma with high tumor burden — Comparator: Historical GALLIUM data — Results: 87% 24-month PFS, 88% CR, manageable toxicity with no ICANS.

Glofitamab-gxbm — Mechanism: CD20×CD3 bispecific antibody — Phase: Bridging strategy analysis — Population: Large B-cell lymphoma (LBCL) — Comparator: Conventional bridging — Results: 69% ORR (BisCAR) vs 61% (ConCAR); 3% grade 3-4 cytopenia vs 22%.

Glofitamab-gxbm + GemOx — Mechanism: CD20×CD3 bispecific + chemotherapy — Phase: Bridging strategy analysis — Population: Large B-cell lymphoma — Comparator: Bispecific monotherapy — Results: 75% ORR and 42% CR during bridging vs 65% ORR and lower CR with monotherapy.

Epcoritamab-bysp — Mechanism: CD3×CD20 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 3 (EPCORE DLBCL-1) — Population: Relapsed/refractory LBCL ineligible for or post-HDT-ASCT — Comparator: Chemoimmunotherapy — Endpoints: Median PFS 3.5 vs 3.0 months (HR 0.74); CR 38% vs 26%; 26% risk reduction.

Anbenitamab (KN026) — Mechanism: HER2 bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 (HEALER, Neo-Healer) — Population: HER2-positive advanced or early breast cancer — Comparator: THP (trastuzumab/pertuzumab/docetaxel) — Endpoints: HEALER PFS met; Neo-Healer tpCR 62.4% vs 51.2% (diff 11.4%).
Bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement — Phase: Varies by program — Population: Lung cancer patients — Context: Emerging as a key competitor to ADCs in lung cancer therapy.
AmMax Bio — Executive leadership appointment — Bispecific ADC portfolio — Appointed Travis Read as President and COO to oversee business development and financing for bispecific ADC programs.

Genmab A/S — Employee warrant exercise — 185 shares issued at DKK 1,334.50/share — Total proceeds DKK 0.2 million — Represents less than 0.001% of total share capital; no impact on bispecific pipeline strategy reported.
Context Therapeutics — Granted non-qualified stock option award to new employee — 146,000 shares at $1.71/share exercise price — Inducement grant under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4) outside 2021 LTIP.

AmMax Bio — Executive appointment — Travis Read (President/COO) — Appointment aimed at guiding transformational growth, business development, and financing for ADC portfolio including bispecific programs.
NovaBridge Biosciences — Collaboration agreement with ABL Bio — Ragistomig (bispecific antibody) — Joint development of PD-L1 x 4-1BB bispecific for solid tumors.
UCB — Acquired Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific oncology assets — Deal value up to $2.2 billion announced in 2026.
UCB — Acquired Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific oncology assets — Deal value up to $2.2 billion; strengthens UCB's oncology portfolio with bispecific technology.
